This file lives under test/fixtures and is support data for other official Node tests, not a top-level runnable compatibility test. It is counted by the broad vendored test-* denominator only because its filename starts with test-.

This official abort fixture asserts host process abort and exit-code behavior. The embedded Node22 runtime lane does not currently own destructive process-abort execution, so it remains a process-host gap rather than a green claim.

This official addon fixture depends on native addon loading in a non-Node context. The current embedded runtime lane does not own native-addon host execution, so it is classified as a loader/native-addon gap rather than an unknown.

This official async-hooks fixture asserts native async resource provider accounting. The current embedded Node22 lane does not yet own FSEVENTWRAP-level provider fidelity, so it is classified as an async-hooks resource-accounting gap.

This official async-hooks fixture asserts native FSREQCALLBACK provider accounting. The current embedded Node22 lane does not yet own filesystem async resource provider fidelity, so it is classified as an async-hooks resource-accounting gap.

This official async-hooks fixture asserts native TTYWRAP provider accounting. The current embedded Node22 lane does not yet own TTY provider-level async resource fidelity, so it is classified as an async-hooks resource-accounting gap.

This file is under upstream Node's known_issues tree and exercises platform-specific filesystem behavior. The current Node22 support claim does not count known-issue platform probes as green without a dedicated owner and measured runtime path.
